danger_signals.visual_form: Animated sprites: Snapjaws (bear-trap eyes), Nitpickers (bird-like creatures), Sparks (electrical hazards on wiring)
danger_signals.meaning: Immediate collision threat; touching enemies causes life loss

danger_signals.visual_form: Vertical screen space below character; gravity-affected movement
danger_signals.meaning: Excessive fall distance causes automatic life loss

danger_signals.visual_form: Stage 1 environment includes water/liquid at bottom sections
danger_signals.meaning: Contact with water results in life loss
Timer reaching zero ends stage with life loss

danger_signals.visual_form: Dropped egg sprites from bird enemies in Stage 2
danger_signals.meaning: Contact with projectiles causes immediate damage/life loss

safety_signals.visual_form: Vertical climbing structures in distinct visual color; characters transition to climbing animation
safety_signals.meaning: Safe traversal path; allows vertical movement and enemy avoidance

safety_signals.visual_form: Horizontal surface sprites with collision detection
safety_signals.meaning: Safe standing surface; allows movement and jumping

safety_signals.visual_form: Colored fruit sprites (apples, grapes, etc.) that fall when knocked down
safety_signals.meaning: Destroys all enemies in contact path before disappearing; provides tactical safety

safety_signals.visual_form: Active countdown timer with positive score values
safety_signals.meaning: Stage progression safe; time remains before penalty

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Caged sprite in enclosure; visual identity as captive father figure
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Cage structure at top of stage; chains securing the cage; stage progression goal

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Character flees when objective reached; represents authority/captor role
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Static or active sprite depending on stage phase
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Hideout location (Stage 4); helicopter transport visual; key placement around building

faction_indicators.color_identity: Smaller brown variant of Donkey Kong
faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Climbing and traversal focus; rescue mission identity
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Starting position; responds to player input; climbs toward caged father

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Natural environment with vines, platforms, water hazards; brown/green color palette
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Snapjaws and Nitpickers spawn; fruit available for knockdown; water at stage bottom

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Mid-level environment with chains; Nitpicker enemies deployed with egg-dropping attacks
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Stage 2-specific enemy behavior; louder Nitpicker audio (Revision 0); fruit knockdown mechanics

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Environmental transition; logical progression toward Mario's hideout
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Increased difficulty; modified enemy spawn rates on higher difficulties

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Interior structure; wiring visible; electrical sparks as environmental hazard; locked containers for keys
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Sparks roam wiring paths; six key locks visible; different goal (push keys into locks vs. reach hanging key)

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Mario sprite animates fleeing; cage pushed off-screen; visual confirmation of success
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Cutscene trigger; transition to next stage at increased difficulty

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Donkey Kong freed from cage; brief cutscene plays
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Game loops back to Stage 1 at increased difficulty; permanent world-state progression

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Enemy spawn rate changes; unused code suggests increased targeting behavior toward player at high difficulty
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Enemies more aggressive in seeking Junior's position when near top of stage

environmental_storytelling.example: Player climbs upward through four distinct stage environments toward caged father at top of screen; visual height progression communicates narrative advancement
Snapjaws in forest (Stage 1), Nitpickers with eggs in vines (Stage 2), Sparks on electrical wiring (Stage 4); enemy variety reflects environmental hazards of each location
Hanging key next to father's cage remains visible throughout stages 1-3; creates persistent visual goal for player navigation
Water hazard at stage bottom; fruit that destroys enemies; chain/vine climbing mechanics—all visually integrated to suggest traversal rules
Mario's sprite animates fleeing when objective reached; cage removed from stage; visual confirmation of antagonist defeat
Stage 4 interior features visible electrical wiring with animated Sparks; communicates different mechanical threat than earlier nature-based stages

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Animation changes to climbing; collision with vine/chain/rope enables upward traversal
Brief arc animation; enables platform-to-platform traversal and enemy dodging
Proximity-triggered; fruit falls and destroys enemy sprites in contact path
Static or minimal animation; cage remains on-screen
Animated flee action; cage pushes off-screen; triggers stage transition
Patrol behavior; collision with Junior causes life loss
Bird-like movement; egg projectile drops when in proximity to Junior
Linear roaming on electrical wiring paths; collision-based threat
Increased spawn frequency; unused code suggests higher targeting probability toward Junior when near top of stage (0x513 behavior parameter, set at 0xB0 = 68.75% normal rate)

notes: Donkey Kong Jr. communicates world-state primarily through vertical spatial progression and environmental hazard placement rather than complex political systems. The narrative is simple: Mario (villain) has caged Donkey Kong (victim); Junior (hero) climbs toward rescue. Visual communication relies on sprite-based character identification, stage-specific enemy/hazard pairing, and objective-marker visibility (the hanging key). The game lacks faction complexity but uses color identity (red Mario vs. brown Kong) for quick antagonist recognition. Behavioral tells are straightforward: fruit knockdown = safety tool, touching enemy = danger, timer depletion = penalty. No faction politics or nuanced world-state representation exists; this is a T2 qualitative analysis of a simple arcade platformer's manifestation layer.
sources_used: tcrf (Cutting Room Floor: Donkey Kong Jr. NES)
